incidents.title: ROUTER-LAB-01 BGP session drop and CPU exhaustion
incidents.symptom_description: ROUTER-LAB-01 experiencing CPU exhaustion at 92% and memory at 95%. BGP session with peer 10.0.0.3 dropped due to hold timer expiry. Interface GigabitEthernet0/2 went DOWN causing cascade failure across downstream devices. bgpd process crashed and required manual restart.
incidents.root_cause: Memory exhaustion caused by traffic spike led to CPU overload. BGP hold timer expired because keepalive packets were not sent in time due to CPU starvation. Interface GigabitEthernet0/2 went down due to link failure compounding the BGP issue.

timeline.event: CPU utilization reached 78% - WARNING threshold crossed
Memory utilization reached 82% - WARNING threshold crossed
Memory utilization reached 95% - CRITICAL threshold crossed
BGP session with peer 10.0.0.3 dropped - hold timer expired
bgpd process failed all restart attempts

incidents.title: ROUTER-LAB-01 BGP flap due to CPU spike
incidents.symptom_description: ROUTER-LAB-01 CPU spiked to 89% during load test causing BGP hold timer to expire. BGP session flapped 3 times within 10 minutes. OSPF adjacency also dropped briefly.
incidents.root_cause: OSPF hello timer set too aggressively at 1 second caused excessive CPU load during high traffic periods. Combined with insufficient memory guard thresholds this led to BGP hold timer expiry.

timeline.event: BGP session dropped - hold timer expired
OSPF hello timer increased to 10 seconds

incidents.resolution: Increased OSPF hello timer from 1 second to 10 seconds. Added memory guard threshold at 85% to trigger garbage collection. Applied BGP graceful-restart configuration. Added bgp timers 30 90 to reduce keepalive frequency.

incidents.title: NET-LAB-ALPHA complete outage due to memory leak
incidents.symptom_description: Complete lab outage caused by memory leak in IOS-XE 17.6.1. Memory grew to 100% over 6 hours causing all routing processes to crash simultaneously.
incidents.root_cause: Known memory leak bug in IOS-XE 17.6.1 affecting ASR 9000 platform. Memory leak in BGP process caused gradual memory exhaustion over 6 hours leading to complete routing process failure.
incidents.resolution: Emergency reboot of ROUTER-LAB-01. Upgraded IOS-XE from 17.6.1 to 17.9.3 to fix memory leak bug. Note: ROUTER-LAB-02 already running 17.9.3 was unaffected.

incidents.title: 5G-UPF-01 crash causing 5G core failure
incidents.symptom_description: 5G-UPF-01 crashed due to CPU and memory exhaustion. N4 interface between SMF and UPF went DOWN. N2 interface between AMF and eNodeB simulator also failed. All 5G test cases blocked.
incidents.root_cause: Resource exhaustion on 5G-UPF-01 caused by excessive PDU sessions during stress test. Nokia AirScale 23.R1.1 has known issue with PDU session cleanup not releasing memory properly.

timeline.event: N2 interface DOWN - eNodeB lost connectivity

incidents.resolution: Restarted 5G-UPF-01 container. Cleared all stale PDU sessions. Applied PDU session limit of 1000 concurrent sessions. Scheduled Nokia patch upgrade for next maintenance window.
resolution_commands: kubectl exec -it upf-01-pod -- upfctl clear-sessions all

incidents.title: SW-LAB-02 DOWN due to JunOS upgrade failure
incidents.symptom_description: SW-LAB-02 went DOWN after JunOS upgrade from 21.2R1 to 21.4R2 failed mid-installation. Device stuck in boot loop.
incidents.root_cause: Incompatible configuration syntax between JunOS 21.2R1 and 21.4R2. Upgrade script did not perform pre-upgrade config validation causing boot failure.

timeline.event: Upgrade failed - device entered boot loop

incidents.resolution: Booted from recovery partition. Rolled back to JunOS 21.2R1. Performed config migration to new syntax before retrying upgrade.
resolution_commands: request system reboot slice alternate media internal
